'''Hric Dalhney''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaanian]] politician who served as [[Deputy Minister of Security]] and was a trusted member of [[Viceroy/Legends|Viceroy]] [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Organa's]] cabinet during the early years of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. He was also part of Alderaan's resistance movement.

==Biography==
Bail Organa sent Dalhney, accompanied by [[Winter Celchu|Winter]] (posing as his daughter) and [[Ylenic It'kla]] to [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] on [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]] to meet with representatives from three anti-Imperial resistance groups: [[Bria Tharen]] and [[Jace Paol]] from [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]], [[Jenssar SoBilles]] from [[Duro/Legends|Duro]] and [[Sian Tevv/Legends|Sian Tevv]] from [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]]. The [[Corellian Resistance/Legends|Corellian Resistance]] was attempting to gather support for unifying the resistance groups in order to better fight the Empire. However, Dalhney was unwilling to go against Alderaan's non-violent culture by committing forces to open rebellion, though he did agree to support the Corellians where possible.

==Personality and traits==
By [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], Dalhney was an older man who sported graying [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and a beard. In keeping with the traditional values of his people, the minister agreed that the Empire should be stopped, but he refused to involve Alderaanians in acts of violence.

==Behind the scenes==
Hric Dalhney first appeared in ''[[Rebel Dawn]]'', a novel by [[A. C. Crispin]]. She [[Tuckerization|named]] the character after ''Star Wars'' author [[Rich Handley]].<ref name="Palpatones">{{Facebook|username=rich.handley.3|url=rich.handley.3/posts/10223283778555809|name=[[Rich Handley]] with [[Tim Pollard]]|date=June 16, 2021 11:45|quote=Here's one of my contributions to the Star Wars franchise that I'm particularly proud of: The Palpatones|archivefile=File:Palpatones-Dahlney-Pollard.png}}</ref>
